# Transcrição: 21. Stop Occupying, Start Working

- **Fonte:** 21. Stop Occupying, Start Working
- **Duração:** 01:40
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:40]

Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. It looks like the occupiers have now gotten tired of the parks and they're moving to the ports. They're in Long Beach this morning. Do you people understand, okay? Other than getting the attention of whoever, okay, me for instance. Do you understand that you are actually costing the middle class worker, the guy that depends on a job, you're costing him, not these billion and trillion dollar companies? Look, you're stopping exports out of the United States and imports into the United States that actually drive business, okay? And driving businesses create incomes for most of the people that want to work and not occupy a port. This is from one of the people that works with me from Chris. Chris says, he's driving through Long Beach and he says, effing protesters, okay, you know what that means. I have no problem with your movement and your right to protest, but now you're affecting the livelihood of 100 plus employees who work for me and the millions in the industry that he works in. Do you think, do you really think shutting down a port or trying to shut down a port is gonna hurt billion and trillion dollar industries and companies? These protesters do not realize how many hardworking Americans that are dependent upon the port in Long Beach in this case for work and some work for small local companies. Do you not have something better to do or you just don't wanna work? Man, quit occupying ports and picnics and smoking weed on a Monday morning when you ought to be working. Get back to work and get that brown stain off your fingers.
